summary: I am Kara Kim Marie Pauly, but before I was known by this name, I was called Kwan Ok Kim. I was born in Seoul, South Korea, on June 1st, 1975, and found abandoned on a street there. After being rescued and cared for at Seoul Municipal Children's Hospital, I was adopted by Myron and Deborah Pauly and came to Minnesota on January 21st, 1976. A few years later, I became a citizen of the United States of America, starting a new life with my family.

My original name was Kwan Ok Kim, and I was born in Seoul, South Korea, in 1975. I was found abandoned and very ill on the streets of Seoul and taken to Seoul Municipal Children's Hospital on August 7th, 1975. While in Korea, I lived with a foster family who took great care of me as my health improved. Eventually, I was adopted by Myron and Deborah Pauly and moved to Minnesota in early 1976, where I was renamed Kara Kim Marie Pauly.

Seoul is where I was born and where my early life unfolded. It was there, on the streets of Seoul, that I was found abandoned and taken to Seoul Municipal Children's Hospital in August 1975. I also lived in a foster home in Seoul before being adopted and leaving for America in January 1976. Seoul holds the beginning of my story and the place where my journey to a new life started.

South Korea is the country where I was born and spent the first months of my life. After being found abandoned in Seoul, South Korea, I was cared for at Seoul Municipal Children's Hospital and then placed with a foster family there. It was through adoption agencies in South Korea that my future family in America connected with me. Eventually, I left South Korea in January 1976 to begin my new life in the United States.

Seoul Municipal Children's Hospital was where I was taken after being found abandoned on the streets of Seoul in August 1975. I was very ill and not expected to survive, but the hospital staff slowly nursed me back to health. It was during my time there that my future adoptive grandparents, Myron and Deborah Pauly, began the adoption process. The hospital was the first place that gave me a chance at life and connected me to my new family.

Myron Pauly is my adoptive grandfather who, along with his wife Deborah, began the process of adopting me from Korea. They received pictures and letters about my health and progress while I was in Seoul Municipal Children's Hospital and a foster home. Myron, Deborah, and their family welcomed me warmly when I arrived in Minnesota in January 1976. They played a crucial role in giving me a new home and family in the United States.

Deborah Pauly is my adoptive grandmother who, with her husband Myron, decided to adopt me after learning about my situation in Korea. They kept in close contact with the adoption agencies and received updates about my health and behavior. When I arrived in Minnesota in January 1976, Deborah was there to welcome me into my new family. She has been a loving presence in my life since that day.

Minnesota is where I arrived when I came to America after being adopted from Korea in January 1976. It was at the Minnesota airport that my new family, including Myron and Deborah Pauly, greeted me with joy. This state became my new home and the place where I began my life as Kara Kim Marie Pauly. Minnesota represents the start of my American journey and the place I grew up.

The United States of America is the country I immigrated to as an infant after being adopted from South Korea. After arriving in Minnesota in early 1976, I was welcomed by my adoptive family and began a new life here. A few years later, I became a U.S. citizen, marking the official start of my life as Kara Kim Marie Pauly. The United States has been my home ever since, where I was raised and grew into who I am today.
